arguments.prompt: analyze physical dimensions of regions of interest in microscopy images

response: ij1-analyze-find-particles2d

Converts mask images into ROI by applying a connected components algorithm and generates measurements. Please note that this algorithm will always trace the external object boundaries and convert them into polygonal ROIs. This means that holes will be closed. This node requires a thresholded image as input and will extract measurements from the thresholded image. If you already have ROI available and want measurements, use 'Extract ROI statistics'.If higher-dimensional data is provided, the results are generated for each 2D slice.

This matrix is then passed to our next node, which is designed to calculate the area of each group and create a matrix of these areas.

Therefore, our next step should involve checking whether the node that calculates the area of each group (and its corresponding matrix of areas) can be connected to the output of the previous node (the matrix of group areas). This will help us identify the best fit node for our needs, as it should have an output that matches the previous node's output in terms of data format and structure.

arguments.prompt: Tracks objects over time and outputs their size and intensity

response: ij1-analyze-find-particles2d

Converts mask images into ROI by applying a connected components algorithm and generates measurements. Please note that this algorithm will always trace the external object boundaries and convert them into polygonal ROIs. This means that holes will be closed. This node requires a thresholded image as input and will extract measurements from the thresholded image. If you already have ROI available and want measurements, use 'Extract ROI statistics'.If higher-dimensional data is provided, the results are generated for each 2D slice.

{"description": "This pipeline processes image data by first converting regions of interest into label images, then removing labels that touch the edges of the image. The resulting labels are converted back into regions of interest and their statistics are calculated. These statistics are then combined into a single table, which can be exported as a file. Additionally, the pipeline generates tables containing properties of specific objects, such as macrophages and spores, excluding those that touch the edges of the image, and exports these tables as files. The pipeline also allows for the export of combined results in a single table. Overall, the pipeline appears to be designed for analyzing and processing microscopic image data, specifically for identifying and characterizing certain types of cells or objects within the images.", "nodes": {"48e06639-9499-47a5-8308-6f376c50fe6b": {"name": "table-merge-tables", "description": "Merges multiple tables into one table by merging the list of rows. Columns are automatically created if they do not exist.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of multiple data items. If you want to group all data into one output, set the matching strategy to 'Custom' and leave 'Data set matching annotations' empty."}, "c622a709-ed7b-4c5f-8f2a-8019e906dc4e": {"name": "ij1-roi-to-labels-expression", "description": "Converts ROI and an optional reference image into a label image. Each ROI is assigned its own label. Alternatively, the label can be set generated from an expression. If no reference image is provided, the dimensions are estimated from the ROI. The background color (where no ROI is located) is zero.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot."}, "527013d5-dd79-4e45-b431-d9753ff3ec5f": {"name": "ij1-roi-statistics", "description": "Generates a results table containing ROI statistics. If a reference image is provided, the statistics are calculated for the reference image. Otherwise, an empty reference image is automatically generated.

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot."}, "643d3826-9c01-489a-9f5d-df31ed94007f": {"name": "clij:plugin:net.haesleinhuepf.clij2.plugins.ExcludeLabelsOnEdges", "description": "Removes all labels from a label map which touch the edges of the image (in X, Y and Z if the image is 3D).

Remaining label elements are renumbered afterwards.

Parameters: Image label_map_input, ByRef Image label_map_destination
Available for: 2D, 3D



Please be aware that this node was generated automatically by JIPipe from existing interfaces provided by CLIJ. Please contact the JIPipe developers if you find any issues."}, "ecc56997-099c-4db7-b73b-ff814407f0de": {"name": "iji-export-table-v2", "description": "Exports a results table to CSV/XLSX

This algorithm groups the incoming data based on the annotations. Those groups can consist of one data item per slot."}, "622cb5f2-a9ef-48f5-9db2-96880aef79b9": {"name": "ij1-roi-to-labels-expression", "description": "Converts ROI and an optional reference image into a label image. Each ROI is assigned its own label. Alternatively, the label can be set generated from an expression. If no reference image is provided, the dimensions are estimated from the ROI. The background color (where no ROI is located) is zero.

Those groups can consist of one data item per slot."}, "cbd810f2-412a-4db2-8437-e28f3c8e7b82": {"name": "ij1-labels-to-roi", "description": "Converts a label image into a set of ROI. Labels must have a value larger than zero to be detected.If higher-dimensional data is provided, the filter is applied to each 2D slice. {"description": "This pipeline imports a model from a file and uses it to run image segmentation on an input image, generating various outputs such as labels, flows, probabilities, and regions of interest, allowing for flexible analysis of biomedical microscopic images in 2D and 3D.", "nodes": {"3ddb0918-f03f-4286-aa48-7902213ef14e": {"name": "import-cellpose-model-v2", "description": "Imports a Cellpose model from a file"}, "56d2d2b2-c091-4b1f-9259-5f545949196d": {"name": "cellpose-segmentation-inference-3.x", "description": "Runs Cellpose on the input image with the given model(s). This node supports both segmentation in 3D and executing Cellpose for each 2D image plane. This node can generate a multitude of outputs, although only ROI is activated by default. Go to the 'Outputs' parameter section to enable the other outputs.
  • Labels: A grayscale image where each connected component is assigned a unique value.
  • Flows XY: An RGB image that indicates the x and y flow of each pixel
  • Flows Z: Flows in Z direction (black for non-3D images)
  • Flows d: Multi-channel image that contains the flows [dZ, dY, dX, cell probability] (3D images) / [dY, dX, cell probability] (2D images)
  • Probabilities: An image indicating the cell probabilities for each pixel
  • ROI: ROI of the segmented areas.
Please note that you need to setup a valid Python environment with Cellpose installed. You can find the setting in Project > Application settings > Extensions > Cellpose.

This algorithm merges all annotations and data annotations. Use the iteration step settings to determine how annotations and data annotations are merged"}}, "edges": [{"source-node": "3ddb0918-f03f-4286-aa48-7902213ef14e", "target-node": "56d2d2b2-c091-4b1f-9259-5f545949196d", "source-slot": "Output", "target-slot": "Model"}]}
